name: code-snippet-debug
purpose: Debugging short functions (<50 LOC) and interpreting error messages. Requires understanding program state and common failure modes across languages.

name: code-generation
purpose: Generating small to medium code from a specification. Tests whether a cheaper model tier can correctly implement a well-specified function vs. needing deeper reasoning.

name: integration-patterns
purpose: Connecting services, API wiring, and config generation tasks. Moderate complexity where the cheapest acceptable route varies by whether the integration has ambiguity.

name: data-transformation
purpose: ETL logic, data reshaping, and format conversion. Debugging and coding prompts that involve structured data rather than open-ended reasoning.

name: architecture-design
purpose: System design, component trade-off analysis, and architecture decision records. High adjudication cost; requires large model generation to produce realistic, non-trivial prompts.

name: security-review
purpose: Threat modeling, secret scanning, auth design review, and security policy analysis. Prompts where insufficient routes produce dangerous outputs -- a core test of the catastrophic under-routing zero-tolerance gate.

name: hard-ambiguity
purpose: Prompts with missing context, conflicting requirements, or underspecified goals. Tests the router's ability to distinguish genuinely hard ambiguity from solvable ambiguity where a cheaper route with clarifying behavior is still acceptable.

name: multi-step-reasoning
purpose: Long-horizon reasoning chains, research synthesis, and multi-part analysis tasks. Distinguishes prompts where the cheapest acceptable route with high effort is sufficient from those that require a stronger model tier.
